ksas.statement: Ability to balance short-term operational data and AI demands against long-term strategic platform investments under resource and organizational constraints.
Ability to prioritize data governance investments across competing data domains and business units with limited resources and uneven organizational maturity.
Ability to distinguish between AI use cases that require formal governance review and those that can proceed under standard operating procedures, calibrating oversight to actual risk.
Ability to sustain governance program momentum in organizations where data governance lacks executive mandate or where business unit resistance is high.
Ability to serve as an effective bridge between business stakeholders who generate and consume data and technical stakeholders who store and process it.

Knowledge of enterprise data strategy frameworks, including data governance operating models, data architecture principles, and the organizational interdependencies that determine data program effectiveness.
Knowledge of enterprise risk management frameworks and how data risk, AI risk, and technology risk are integrated into enterprise risk reporting and board-level risk governance.
Knowledge of data governance operating models, including federated, centralized, and hub-and-spoke structures, and the organizational conditions under which each model is appropriate.
Knowledge of data catalog and metadata management platforms, including active metadata principles, automated lineage tracking, and business glossary governance.
Knowledge of data maturity assessment frameworks, including DCAM, DAMA DMBOK, and CMMI-DMM, and how maturity scores translate to organizational investment priorities.
Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift detection, performance monitoring, retraining triggers, and decommissioning criteria.
Knowledge of data governance program design, including operating model options, council structures, policy frameworks, stewardship network models, and escalation path architectures.
Knowledge of data policy authoring, including policy structure standards, enforcement mechanisms, exception handling processes, and policy lifecycle management.
Knowledge of data domain and data ownership concepts, including how to define domain boundaries, assign accountability, and resolve cross-domain ownership disputes.
Knowledge of the data domain for which stewardship responsibility is held, including its business purpose, system sources, consumer use cases, and applicable regulatory classifications.
Knowledge of metadata standards, business glossary conventions, and data catalog tooling used to document and publish data asset definitions.
Skill in communicating data and AI strategy, risk posture, and investment priorities to board-level and C-suite audiences using financial and business terms rather than technical language.
Skill in aligning data and AI program objectives to organizational strategy, translating business priorities into data and AI capability requirements with measurable outcomes.
Skill in designing data stewardship programs that assign clear ownership, accountability, and service level agreements for enterprise data domains.
Skill in building and managing a Data Governance Council, including membership design, decision authority, meeting cadence, escalation processes, and conflict resolution.
Skill in operating data governance councils, including agenda management, issue escalation, decision documentation, and multi-stakeholder conflict resolution.
Skill in designing and running data stewardship programs, including steward selection, training, responsibility definition, and performance measurement.
Skill in measuring and reporting data governance program effectiveness using maturity scores, policy compliance rates, issue resolution metrics, and stewardship participation rates.
Skill in authoring and maintaining business glossary definitions, data dictionaries, and data asset documentation that are clear and accurate for both technical and business audiences.
Skill in developing actionable data and AI strategy recommendations, including prioritized roadmaps, capability investment plans, and organizational design recommendations.
Manage the enterprise business glossary, including term authoring workflows, approval processes, linkage to catalog assets, and conflict resolution between competing definitions.
Develop and maintain the enterprise data and AI strategy, aligned to business objectives, with defined investment priorities, milestones, accountability assignments, and success metrics.
Lead selection, contracting, and performance oversight of strategic data and AI technology vendors and platform providers.
Establish and operate the enterprise data governance program, including council structure, policy library, stewardship network, data domain ownership registry, and governance metrics.
Conduct enterprise data maturity assessments against recognized frameworks and define improvement roadmaps with measurable milestones and accountable owners.
Establish and maintain the enterprise data catalog, business glossary, and metadata management program, ensuring discoverability, lineage visibility, and classification currency.
Establish post-deployment AI monitoring standards, including performance threshold definitions, drift detection protocols, incident reporting procedures, and remediation timelines.
Operate the Data Governance Council, including scheduling, agenda preparation, issue tracking, decision logging, and follow-through on action items.
Manage the enterprise data stewardship network, including steward onboarding, role clarity, tools access, and regular steward community engagement.
Produce and distribute regular data governance program status reports, including maturity progress, open issues, policy violations, and upcoming regulatory obligations.
Maintain the business glossary definitions, data asset catalog entries, and data lineage documentation for the assigned data domain.
Participate in the data governance council and stewardship community, representing the assigned domain's interests and escalating unresolved issues through established channels.
Review and approve data access requests, data sharing agreements, and data use authorizations for data assets within the assigned domain.
Conduct data and AI strategy engagements, including current state assessment, future state design, gap analysis, and roadmap development with defined milestones and investment estimates.
Process data access requests per defined procedures, including request validation, approval routing, provisioning, and access log documentation.
